text: The surgeon retracts the sigmoid colon and scores the peritoneum to the right of the colorectal junction using monopolar electrocautery. The right ureter is identified with endocyanine green injection, and care is taken to avoid the hypogastric nerves.
The surgeon develops the holy plane posterior to the mesorectum and anterior to the presacral fascia, carefully separating these anatomical layers.
The surgeon begins anterior dissection by scoring the peritoneum between the urinary bladder and the rectum. As the dissection deepens, the gastrointestinal stromal tumor is visually identified.

The assistant performs a digital rectal exam to verify the depth of dissection within the pelvis. The tumor is carefully freed from its adhesion to the rectum, ensuring the capsule of the tumor remains intact.
The surgeon uses sharp dissection to free the tumor from its adhesion to the prostate, further mobilizing it off the pelvic floor.
